req.title: Lab order routing
req.statement: A clinician shall order a lab test; orders shall route to the patient's preferred lab when available.
error_pathways: fallback to default lab when preferred unavailable

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
```python
"""
Pytest tests for lab test ordering and routing to patient's preferred lab.

Requirement: A clinician shall order a lab test; orders shall route to the
patient's preferred lab when available.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional
from enum import Enum


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain models (stubs – replace with real imports in your project)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class OrderStatus(Enum):
    PENDING = "PENDING"
    ROUTED = "ROUTED"
    FAILED = "FAILED"
    REJECTED = "REJECTED"


class LabStatus(Enum):
    ACTIVE = "ACTIVE"
    INACTIVE = "INACTIVE"
    SUSPENDED = "SUSPENDED"


@dataclass
class Lab:
    lab_id: str
    name: str
    status: LabStatus = LabStatus.ACTIVE
    supported_tests: list = field(default_factory=list)


@dataclass
class Patient:
    patient_id: str
    name: str
    preferred_lab: Optional[Lab] = None


@dataclass
class Clinician:
    clinician_id: str
    name: str
    is_licensed: bool = True


@dataclass
class LabTest:
    test_code: str
    test_name: str


@dataclass
class LabOrder:
    order_id: str
    patient: Patient
    clinician: Clinician
    test: LabTest
    status: OrderStatus = OrderStatus.PENDING
    routed_to_lab: Optional[Lab] = None
    rejection_reason: Optional[str] = None


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Service interfaces (stubs – replace with real imports in your project)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class LabOrderService:
    """Service responsible for creating and routing lab orders."""

    def __init__(self, lab_repository, notification_service, audit_service):
        self.lab_repository = lab_repository
        self.notification_service = notification_service
        self.audit_service = audit_service

    def create_order(
        self,
        clinician: Clinician,
        patient: Patient,
        test: LabTest,
        order_id: str = "ORD-001",
    ) -> LabOrder:
        if not clinician.is_licensed:
            order = LabOrder(
                order_id=order_id,
                patient=patient,
                clinician=clinician,
                test=test,
                status=OrderStatus.REJECTED,
                rejection_reason="Clinician is not licensed",
            )
            return order

        order = LabOrder(
            order_id=order_id,
            patient=patient,
            clinician=clinician,
            test=test,
        )
        self._route_order(order)
        return order

    def _route_order(self, order: LabOrder) -> None:
        preferred_lab = order.patient.preferred_lab

        if preferred_lab and preferred_lab.status == LabStatus.ACTIVE:
            if order.test.test_code in preferred_lab.supported_tests:
                order.routed_to_lab = preferred_lab
                order.status = OrderStatus.ROUTED
                self.notification_service.notify_lab(order)
                self.audit_service.log_routing(order)
                return

        # Fallback: find any available lab that supports the test
        fallback_lab = self.lab_repository.find_available_lab(order.test.test_code)
        if fallback_lab:
            order.routed_to_lab = fallback_lab
            order.status = OrderStatus.ROUTED
            self.notification_service.notify_lab(order)
            self.audit_service.log_routing(order)
        else:
            order.status = OrderStatus.FAILED
            order.rejection_reason = "No available lab for the requested test"
            self.audit_service.log_routing(order)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def lab_repository():
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture
def notification_service():
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture
def audit_service():
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture
def order_service(lab_repository, notification_service, audit_service):
    return LabOrderService(
        lab_repository=lab_repository,
        notification_service=notification_service,
        audit_service=audit_service,
    )


@pytest.fixture
def cbc_test():
    return LabTest(test_code="CBC", test_name="Complete Blood Count")


@pytest.fixture
def lipid_test():
    return LabTest(test_code="LIPID", test_name="Lipid Panel")


@pytest.fixture
def preferred_lab():
    return Lab(
        lab_id="LAB-001",
        name="Preferred Lab",
        status=LabStatus.ACTIVE,
        supported_tests=["CBC", "LIPID", "HBA1C"],
    )


@pytest.fixture
def fallback_lab():
    return Lab(
        lab_id="LAB-002",
        name="Fallback Lab",
        status=LabStatus.ACTIVE,
        supported_tests=["CBC", "LIPID"],
    )


@pytest.fixture
def patient_with_preferred_lab(preferred_lab):
    return Patient(
        patient_id="PAT-001",
        name="Jane Doe",
        preferred_lab=preferred_lab,
    )


@pytest.fixture
def patient_without_preferred_lab():
    return Patient(
        patient_id="PAT-002",
        name="John Smith",
        preferred_lab=None,
    )


@pytest.fixture
def licensed_clinician():
    return Clinician(
        clinician_id="CLIN-001",
        name="Dr. Alice",
        is_licensed=True,
    )


@pytest.fixture
def unlicensed_clinician():
    return Clinician(
        clinician_id="CLIN-002",
        name="Dr. Bob (Unlicensed)",
        is_licensed=False,
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Tests – Order Creation
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestLabOrderCreation:
    """Tests verifying that a clinician can create a lab order."""

    def test_licensed_clinician_can_create_order(
        self, order_service, licensed_clinician, patient_with_preferred_lab, cbc_test
    ):
        """A licensed clinician shall be able to place a lab order."""
        order = order_service.create_order(
            clinician=licensed_clinician,
            patient=patient_with_preferred_lab,
            test=cbc_test,
        )

        assert order is not None
        assert order.clinician == licensed_clinician
        assert order.patient == patient_with_preferred_lab
        assert order.test == cbc_test

    def test_order_contains_correct_test_details(
        self, order_service, licensed_clinician, patient_with_preferred_lab, cbc_test
    ):
        """The order shall capture the correct test requested by the clinician."""
        order = order_service.create_order(
            clinician=licensed_clinician,
            patient=patient_with_preferred_lab,
            test=cbc_test,
        )

        assert order.test.test_code == "CBC"
        assert order.test.test_name == "Complete Blood Count"

    def test_order_contains_correct_patient(
        self, order_service, licensed_clinician, patient_with_preferred_lab, cbc_test
    ):
        """The order shall be associated with the correct patient."""
        order = order_service.create_order(
            clinician=licensed_clinician,
            patient=patient_with_preferred_lab,
            test=cbc_test,
        )

        assert order.patient.patient_id == "PAT-001"

    def test_unlicensed_clinician_order_is_rejected(
        self,
        order_service,
        unlicensed_clinician,
        patient_with_preferred_lab,
        cbc_test,
    ):
        """An order from an unlicensed clinician shall be rejected."""
        order = order_service.create_order(
            clinician=unlicensed_clinician,
            patient=patient_with_preferred_lab,
            test=cbc_test,
        )

        assert order.status == OrderStatus.REJECTED

    def test_unlicensed_clinician_rejection_reason_populated(
        self,
        order_service,
        unlicensed_clinician,
        patient_with_preferred_lab,
        cbc_test,
    ):
        """A rejected order shall include a human-readable rejection reason."""
        order = order_service.create_order(
            clinician=unlicensed_clinician,
            patient=patient_with_preferred_lab,
            test=cbc_test,
        )

        assert order.rejection_reason is not None
        assert len(order.rejection_reason) > 0

    def test_order_id_is_assigned(
        self, order_service, licensed_clinician, patient_with_preferred_lab, cbc_test
    ):
        """Every order shall be assigned a unique identifier."""
        order = order_service.create_order(
            clinician=licensed_clinician,
            patient=patient_with_preferred_lab,
            test=cbc_test,
            order_id="ORD-XYZ",
        )

        assert order.order_id == "ORD-XYZ"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Tests – Routing to Preferred Lab
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestRoutingToPreferredLab:
    """Tests verifying orders route to the patient's preferred lab when available."""

    def test_order_routes_to_preferred_lab(
        self,
        order_service,
        licensed_clinician,
        patient_with_preferred_lab,
        cbc_test,
        preferred_lab,
    ):
        """When the preferred lab is active and supports the test, the order
        shall be routed there."""
        order = order_service.create_order(
            clinician=licensed_clinician,
            patient=patient_with_preferred_lab,
            test=cbc_test,
        )

        assert order.routed_to_lab == preferred_lab

    def test_order_status_is_routed_when_preferred_lab_used(
        self,
        order_service,
        licensed_clinician,
        patient_with_preferred_lab,
        cbc_test,
    ):
        """An order routed to the preferred lab shall have ROUTED status."""
        order = order_service.create_order(
            clinician=licensed_clinician,
            patient=patient_with_preferred_lab,
            test=cbc_test,
        )

        assert order.status == OrderStatus.ROUTED

    def test_preferred_lab_receives_notification(
        self,
        order_service,
        notification_service,
        licensed_clinician,
        patient_with_preferred_lab,
        cbc_test,
    ):
        """The preferred lab shall be notified when an order is routed to it."""
        order = order_service.create_order(
            clinician=licensed_clinician,
            patient=patient_with_preferred_lab,
            test=cbc_test,
        )

        notification_service.notify_lab.assert_called_once_with(order)

    def test_routing_event_is_audited(
        self,
        order_service,
        audit_service,
        licensed_clinician,
        patient_with_preferred_lab,
        cbc_test,
    ):
        """Every routing decision shall be recorded in the audit log."""
        order = order_service.create_order(
            clinician=licensed_clinician,
            patient=patient_with_preferred_lab,
            test=cbc_test,
        )

        audit_service.log_routing.assert_called_once_with(order)

    def test_preferred_lab_used_for_multiple_supported_tests(
        self,
        order_service,
        licensed_clinician,
        patient_with_preferred_lab,
        preferred_lab,
        lipid_test,
    ):
        """Preferred lab routing shall work for any test the lab supports."""
        order = order_service.create_order(
            clinician=licensed_clinician,
            patient=patient_with_preferred_lab,
            test=lipid_test,
        )

        assert order.routed_to_lab == preferred_lab
        assert order.status == OrderStatus.ROUTED


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Tests – Fallback Routing (Preferred Lab Unavailable)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestFallbackRouting:
    """Tests verifying fallback behaviour when the preferred lab is unavailable."""

    def test_order_routes_to_fallback_when_patient_has_no_preferred_lab(
        self,
        order_service,
        lab_repository,
        licensed_clinician,
        patient_without_preferred_lab,
        cbc_test,
        fallback_lab,
    ):
        """When the patient has no preferred lab, the order shall route to an
        available fallback lab."""
        lab_repository.find_available_lab.return_value = fallback_lab

        order = order_service.create_order(
            clinician=licensed_clinician,
            patient=patient_without_preferred_lab,
            test=cbc_test,
        )

        assert order.routed_to_lab == fallback_lab
        assert order.status == OrderStatus.ROUTED

    def test_order_routes_to_fallback_when_preferred_lab_is_inactive(
        self,
        order_service,
        lab_repository,
        licensed_clinician,
        fallback_lab,
        cbc_test,
    ):
        """When the preferred lab is inactive, the order shall route to a
        fallback lab."""
        inactive_preferred = Lab(
            lab_id="LAB-INACTIVE",
            name="Inactive Preferred Lab",
            status=LabStatus.INACTIVE,
            supported_tests=["CBC"],
        )
        patient = Patient(
            patient_id="PAT-003",
            name="Patient C",
            preferred_lab=inactive_preferred,
        )
        lab_repository.find_available_lab.return_value = fallback_lab

        order = order_service.create_order(
            clinician=licensed_clinician,
            patient=patient,
            test=cbc_test,
        )

        assert order.routed_to_lab == fallback_lab
        assert order.status == OrderStatus.ROUTED

    def test_order_routes_to_fallback_when_preferred_lab_is_suspended(
        self,
        order_service,
        lab_repository,
        licensed_clinician,
        fallback_lab,
        cbc_test,
    ):
        """When the preferred lab is suspended, the order shall route to a
        fallback lab."""
        suspended_preferred = Lab(
            lab_id="LAB-SUSPENDED",
            name="Suspended Preferred Lab",
            status=LabStatus.SUSPENDED,
            supported_tests=["CBC"],
        )
        patient = Patient(
            patient_id="PAT-004",
            name="Patient D",
            preferred_lab=suspended_preferred,
        )
        lab_repository.find_available_lab.return_value = fallback_lab

        order = order_service.create_order(
            clinician=licensed_clinician,
            patient=patient,
            test=cbc_test,
        )

        assert order.routed_to_lab == fallback_lab
        assert order.status == OrderStatus.ROUTED

    def test_order_routes_to_fallback_when_preferred_lab_does_not_support_test(
        self,
        order_service,
        lab_repository,
        licensed_clinician,
        fallback_lab,
    ):
        """When the preferred lab does not support the ordered test, the order
        shall route to a fallback lab that does."""
        restricted_preferred = Lab(
            lab_id="LAB-LIMITED",
            name="Limited Preferred Lab",
            status=LabStatus.ACTIVE,
            supported_tests=["HBA1C"],  # Does NOT support CBC
        )
        patient = Patient(
            patient_id="PAT-005",
            name="Patient E",
            preferred_lab=restricted_preferred,
        )
        unsupported_test = LabTest(test_code="CBC", test_name="Complete Blood Count")
        lab_repository.find_available_lab.return_value = fallback_lab

        order = order_service.create_order(
            clinician=licensed_clinician,
            patient=patient,
            test=unsupported_test,
        )

        assert order.routed_to_lab == fallback_lab
        assert order.routed_to_lab != restricted_preferred

    def test_preferred_lab_not_contacted_when_inactive(
        self,
        order_service,
        lab_repository,
        notification_service,
        licensed_clinician,
        fallback_lab,
        cbc_test,
    ):
        """An inactive preferred lab shall not receive order notifications."""
        inactive_preferred = Lab(
            lab_id="LAB-INACTIVE",
            name="Inactive Preferred Lab",
            status=LabStatus.INACTIVE,
            supported_tests=["CBC"],
        )
        patient = Patient(
            patient_id="PAT-006",
            name="Patient F",
            preferred_lab=inactive_preferred,
        )
        lab_repository.find_available_lab.return_value = fallback_lab

        order = order_service.create_order(
            clinician=licensed_clinician,
            patient=patient,
            test=cbc_test,
        )

        # Notification should go to fallback, not the inactive preferred lab
        notified_order = notification_service.notify_lab.call_args[0][0]
        assert notified_order.routed_to_lab != inactive_preferred

    def test_fallback_lab_lookup_uses_correct_test_code(
        self,
        order_service,
        lab_repository,
        licensed_clinician,
        patient_without_preferred_lab,
        cbc_test,
        fallback_lab,
    ):
        """The fallback lab lookup shall query by the correct test code."""
        lab_repository.find_available_lab.return_value = fallback_lab

        order_service.create_order(
            clinician=licensed_clinician,
            patient=patient_without_preferred_lab,
            test=cbc_test,
        )

        lab_repository.find_available_lab.assert_called_once_with("CBC")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Tests – No Lab Available
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestNoLabAvailable:
    """Tests for the edge case where no lab can fulfil the order."""

    def test_order_fails_when_no_lab_available(
        self,
        order_service,
        lab_repository,
        licensed_clinician,
        patient_without_preferred_lab,
        cbc_test,
    ):
        """When no lab is available for the requested test, the order status
        shall be FAILED."""
        lab_repository.find_available_lab.return_value = None

        order = order_service.create_order(
            clinician=licensed_clinician,
            patient=patient_without_preferred_lab,
            test=cbc_test,
        )

        assert order.status == OrderStatus.FAILED

    def test_failed_order_has_no_routed_lab(
        self,
        order_service,
        lab_repository,
        licensed_clinician,
        patient_without_preferred_lab,
        cbc_test,
    ):
        """A failed order shall not be associated with any lab."""
        lab_repository.find_available_lab.return_value = None

        order = order_service.create_order(
            clinician=licensed_clinician,
            patient=patient_without_preferred_lab,
            test=cbc_test,
        )

        assert order.routed_to_lab is None

    def test_failed_order_has_rejection_reason(
        self,
        order_service,
        lab_repository,
        licensed_clinician,
        patient_without_preferred_lab,
        cbc_test,
    ):
        """A failed order shall include a reason describing why routing failed."""
        lab_repository.find_available_lab.return_value = None

        order = order_service.create_order(
            clinician=licensed_clinician,
            patient=patient_without_preferred_lab,
            test=cbc_test,
        )

        assert order.rejection_reason is not None
        assert len(order.rejection_reason) > 0

    def test_failed_order_is_audited(
        self,
        order_service,
        lab_repository,
        audit_service,
        licensed_clinician,
        patient_without_preferred_lab,
        cbc_test,
    ):
        """A failed routing attempt shall still be recorded in the audit log."""
        lab_repository.find_available_lab.return_value = None

        order = order_service.create_order(
            clinician=licensed_clinician,
            patient=patient_without_preferred_lab,
            test=cbc_test,
        )

        audit_service.log_routing.assert_called_once_with(order)

    def test_no_notification_sent_when_order_fails(
        self,
        order_service,
        lab_repository,
        notification_service,
        licensed_clinician,
        patient_without_preferred_lab,
        cbc_test,
    ):
        """No lab notification shall be sent when no routing destination exists."""
        lab_repository.find_available_lab.return_value = None

        order_service.create_order(
            clinician=licensed_clinician,
            patient=patient_without_preferred_lab,
            test=cbc_test,
        )

        notification_service.notify_lab.assert_not_called()


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Tests – Routing Priority
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestRoutingPriority:
    """Tests confirming preferred lab takes priority over the fallback lab."""

    def test_preferred_lab_takes_priority_over_fallback(
        self,
        order_service,
        lab_repository,
        licensed_clinician,
        patient_with_preferred_lab,
        cbc_test,
        preferred_lab,
        fallback_lab,
    ):
        """The preferred lab shall be chosen even when a fallback lab exists."""
        lab_repository.find_available_lab.return_value = fallback_lab

        order = order_service.create_order(
            clinician=licensed_clinician,
            patient=patient_with_preferred_lab,
            test=cbc_test,
        )

        assert order.routed_to_lab == preferred_lab
        assert order.routed_to_lab != fallback_lab

    def test_fallback_lab_not_queried_when_preferred_lab_used(
        self,
        order_service,
        lab_repository,
        licensed_clinician,
        patient_with_preferred_lab,
        cbc_test,
    ):
        """The lab repository shall not be queried when the preferred lab is
        used, avoiding unnecessary lookups."""
        order_service.create_order(
            clinician=licensed_clinician,
            patient=patient_with_preferred_lab,
            test=cbc_test,
        )

        lab_repository.find_available_lab.assert_not_called()
```

---

## Design Decisions & Coverage Summary

| Category | Tests |
|---|---|
| **Order Creation** | Licensed clinician creates order; order captures correct patient, test, ID; unlicensed clinician is rejected with a reason |
| **Preferred Lab Routing** | Routes to preferred lab when active & test supported; status = ROUTED; lab notified; event audited; works across multiple tests |
| **Fallback Routing** | Routes to fallback when: no preference, lab inactive, lab suspended, lab doesn't support test; inactive lab not notified; correct test code used in lookup |
| **No Lab Available** | Status = FAILED; no lab assigned; rejection reason populated; event audited; no notification sent |
| **Routing Priority** | Preferred lab wins over fallback; repository not queried when preferred lab used |

### Key engineering choices
- **Mocks** for the repository, notification, and audit services — tests remain isolated and fast with no infrastructure needed.  
- **Fixtures** are small and composable; each test only pulls in what it needs.  
- **One assertion per test** principle is followed where practical, so failures give a precise signal.  
- Tests are written against the *behaviour* described in the requirement, not against implementation details, making them robust to refactoring.

"""
Pytest tests for lab test ordering and routing to patient's preferred lab.

Requirement: A clinician shall order a lab test; orders shall route to the
patient's preferred lab when available.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional
from enum import Enum


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain models (stubs – replace with real imports in your project)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class OrderStatus(Enum):
    PENDING = "PENDING"
    ROUTED = "ROUTED"
    FAILED = "FAILED"
    REJECTED = "REJECTED"


class LabStatus(Enum):
    ACTIVE = "ACTIVE"
    INACTIVE = "INACTIVE"
    SUSPENDED = "SUSPENDED"


@dataclass
class Lab:
    lab_id: str
    name: str
    status: LabStatus = LabStatus.ACTIVE
    supported_tests: list = field(default_factory=list)


@dataclass
class Patient:
    patient_id: str
    name: str
    preferred_lab: Optional[Lab] = None


@dataclass
class Clinician:
    clinician_id: str
    name: str
    is_licensed: bool = True


@dataclass
class LabTest:
    test_code: str
    test_name: str


@dataclass
class LabOrder:
    order_id: str
    patient: Patient
    clinician: Clinician
    test: LabTest
    status: OrderStatus = OrderStatus.PENDING
    routed_to_lab: Optional[Lab] = None
    rejection_reason: Optional[str] = None


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Service interfaces (stubs – replace with real imports in your project)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class LabOrderService:
    """Service responsible for creating and routing lab orders."""

    def __init__(self, lab_repository, notification_service, audit_service):
        self.lab_repository = lab_repository
        self.notification_service = notification_service
        self.audit_service = audit_service

    def create_order(
        self,
        clinician: Clinician,
        patient: Patient,
        test: LabTest,
        order_id: str = "ORD-001",
    ) -> LabOrder:
        if not clinician.is_licensed:
            order = LabOrder(
                order_id=order_id,
                patient=patient,
                clinician=clinician,
                test=test,
                status=OrderStatus.REJECTED,
                rejection_reason="Clinician is not licensed",
            )
            return order

        order = LabOrder(
            order_id=order_id,
            patient=patient,
            clinician=clinician,
            test=test,
        )
        self._route_order(order)
        return order

    def _route_order(self, order: LabOrder) -> None:
        preferred_lab = order.patient.preferred_lab

        if preferred_lab and preferred_lab.status == LabStatus.ACTIVE:
            if order.test.test_code in preferred_lab.supported_tests:
                order.routed_to_lab = preferred_lab
                order.status = OrderStatus.ROUTED
                self.notification_service.notify_lab(order)
                self.audit_service.log_routing(order)
                return

        # Fallback: find any available lab that supports the test
        fallback_lab = self.lab_repository.find_available_lab(order.test.test_code)
        if fallback_lab:
            order.routed_to_lab = fallback_lab
            order.status = OrderStatus.ROUTED
            self.notification_service.notify_lab(order)
            self.audit_service.log_routing(order)
        else:
            order.status = OrderStatus.FAILED
            order.rejection_reason = "No available lab for the requested test"
            self.audit_service.log_routing(order)
